content.text: I'll start by understanding the code before touching anything.

input.command: ls test && echo "---" && cat test/*.js 2>/dev/null | head -200

content.text: The cause is clear: the cache key `users:${opts.team}` ignores `includeArchived`, so a result cached for `includeArchived:true` is served to a later `includeArchived:false` request — until the 60s TTL expires (matching "refreshing after a minute clears it"). Let me write a repro test that fails for this reason first.

content.text: The repro fails for exactly the reported reason: the archived-included list is served to the picker's `includeArchived:false` request. Now the minimal fix — include `includeArchived` in the cache key.

content.content: The file /Users/sungjh/Projects/skill-receipts/bench/.workspaces/repro-first-03-cache-key-leak-on-CHwgWH/src/userService.js has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
Fixed. All tests pass, including the new regression guard.

**Mechanism:** `getUsers` built its cache key from `opts.team` only, ignoring `opts.includeArchived`. So an earlier `{includeArchived:true}` call cached an archived-inclusive list under `users:eng`, and the picker's `{includeArchived:false}` call got a cache hit on that same key and received the archived users it never asked for — until the 60s TTL expired, which is why refreshing after a minute cleared it. Adding `includeArchived` to the key (`users:eng:0` vs `users:eng:1`) gives the two request shapes separate cache entries.

The change is one line in `src/userService.js:10`; `cache.js` and the existing test were left untouched.

result:
Fixed. All tests pass, including the new regression guard.

**Mechanism:** `getUsers` built its cache key from `opts.team` only, ignoring `opts.includeArchived`. So an earlier `{includeArchived:true}` call cached an archived-inclusive list under `users:eng`, and the picker's `{includeArchived:false}` call got a cache hit on that same key and received the archived users it never asked for — until the 60s TTL expired, which is why refreshing after a minute cleared it. Adding `includeArchived` to the key (`users:eng:0` vs `users:eng:1`) gives the two request shapes separate cache entries.

The change is one line in `src/userService.js:10`; `cache.js` and the existing test were left untouched.
